key_insight: A coordination-sensitive atom probe approach shows that Si doping plus higher Al content drives local cation coordination loss, triggering gamma-phase inclusions in beta-(AlxGa1-x)2O3.
engineering_relevance: The results provide a nanoscale diagnostic for predicting defect-driven phase instability in ultrawide bandgap oxides, helping guide composition and doping choices for more reliable devices.
method: Coordination-sensitive atom probe tomography to map local chemistry and cation coordination.
Nearest-neighbor and radial distribution analyses to quantify first-shell coordination loss.
Correlative STEM plus DFT to validate the transformation mechanism.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports gamma-phase inclusions only under high Al and heavy Si doping, with 2D compositional inhomogeneity and reduced Ga coordination confirmed by microscopy and supported by DFT.
watch_item: The coordination-loss signature may be system- and processing-dependent, so broader validation is needed before using it as a universal instability predictor.
paper_title: Coordination-Sensitive Nanoscale Analysis of Defect-Driven Phase Transformation in Si-Doped (AlXGa1-X)2O3
